sourceQuestion:
Passage Reading: GATE2011 AG: GA-10

The horse has played a little known but very important role in the field of medicine. Horses were injected with toxins of diseases until their blood built up immunities. Then a serum was made from their blood. Serums to fight with diphtheria and tetanus were developed this way.

It can be inferred from the passage that horses were used to

A. give immunity to diseases
B. generally give immune to diseases
C. given medicines to fight toxins
D. given diphtheria and tetanus serums
sourceAnswer: A. give immunity to diseases

Passage Reading: GATE2011 AG: GA-10

It can be inferred from the passage that horses were used to

options.text: give immunity to diseases
generally give immune to diseases
given medicines to fight toxins
given diphtheria and tetanus serums

sourceQuestion:
Passage Reading: GATE2011 GG: GA-10

In order to develop to full potential, a baby needs to be physically able to respond to the environment.

It can be inferred from the passage that

A. Full physical potential is needed in order for a baby to be able to respond to the environment.
B. It is necessary for a baby to be able to physically respond to the environment for it to develop its full potential.
C. It is good for baby to physically respond to its environment and for it to develop to its full potential.
D. A physically able baby needs to develop its potential in order to respond to its environment.
sourceAnswer: B. It is necessary for a baby to be able to physically respond to the environment for it to develop its full potential.

It is necessary for a baby to be able to physically respond to the environment for it to develop its full potential.
It is good for baby to physically respond to its environment and for it to develop to its full potential.
A physically able baby needs to develop its potential in order to respond to its environment.

sourceQuestion:
Passage Reading: GATE2011 MN: GA-05

Nimbus clouds are dark and ragged, stratus clouds appear dull in colour and cover the entire sky. Cirrus clouds are thin and delicate, whereas cumulus clouds look like cotton balls.

It can be inferred from the passage that

A. A cumulus cloud on the ground is called fog.
B. It is easy to predict the weather by studying clouds.
C. Clouds are generally of different shapes, sizes and mass.
D. There are four basic cloud types: stratus, nimbus, cumulus and cirrus.
sourceAnswer: D. There are four basic cloud types: stratus, nimbus, cumulus and cirrus.

It can be inferred from the passage that

options.text: A cumulus cloud on the ground is called fog.
It is easy to predict the weather by studying clouds.
Clouds are generally of different shapes, sizes and mass.
There are four basic cloud types: stratus, nimbus, cumulus and cirrus.

sourceQuestion:
Passage Reading: GATE2012 AE: GA-10

In the early nineteenth century, theories of social evolution were inspired less by Biology than by the conviction of social scientists that there was a growing improvement in social institutions. Progress was taken for granted and social scientists attempted to discover its laws and phases.

Which one of the following inferences may be drawn with the greatest accuracy from the above passage?

A. Social scientists did not approve of Biology.
B. Social scientists did not approve of Biology.
C. Theories of social evolution were inspired more by social scientists than by Biology.
D. Emphasized Biology over Social Sciences.
sourceAnswer: A. Social scientists did not approve of Biology.

Which one of the following inferences may be drawn with the greatest accuracy from the above passage?

options.text: Social scientists did not approve of Biology.
Social scientists did not approve of Biology.
Theories of social evolution were inspired more by social scientists than by Biology.
Emphasized Biology over Social Sciences.

sourceQuestion:
Passage Reading: GATE2012 AR: GA-10

The documents expose the cynicism of the government officials - and yet as the media website reflects, not a single newspaper has reported on their existence.

Which one of the following inferences may be drawn with the greatest accuracy from the above passage?

A. Nobody other than the government officials knew about the existence of the documents.
B. Newspapers did report about the government officials but nobody cared.
C. Media reports did not show the existence of the documents.
D. The documents reveal the attitude of the government officials.
sourceAnswer: D. The documents reveal the attitude of the government officials.

Newspapers did report about the government officials but nobody cared.
Media reports did not show the existence of the documents.
The documents reveal the attitude of the government officials.

sourceQuestion:
One of the legacies of the Roman legions was discipline. In the legions, military law prevailed and discipline was brutal. Discipline on the battlefield kept units obedient, intact and fighting, even when the odds and conditions were against them.

Which one of the following statements best sums up the meaning of the above passage?

A. Through regimentation was the main reason for the efficiency of the Roman legions even in adverse circumstances.
B. The legions were trained to strictly as per the Roman ways.
C. Discipline was the armies' inheritance from their seniors.
D. The harsh discipline to which the legions were subjected to led to the odds and conditions being against them.
sourceAnswer: A. Through regimentation was the main reason for the efficiency of the Roman legions even in adverse circumstances.

One of the legacies of the Roman legions was discipline. In the legions, military law prevailed and discipline was brutal. Discipline on the battlefield kept units obedient, intact and fighting, even when the odds and conditions were against them.

Which one of the following statements best sums up the meaning of the above passage?

options.text: Through regimentation was the main reason for the efficiency of the Roman legions even in adverse circumstances.
The legions were trained to strictly as per the Roman ways.
Discipline was the armies' inheritance from their seniors.
The harsh discipline to which the legions were subjected to led to the odds and conditions being against them.

sourceQuestion:
Rajan was not happy that Sajan decided to do the project on his own. On observing his unhappiness, Sajan explained to Rajan that he preferred to work independently.

Which one of the statements below is logically valid and can be inferred from the above sentences?

A. Rajan has decided to work only in a group.
B. Rajan and Sajan were formed into a group against their wishes.
C. Sajan had decided to give a part of the project to Rajan.
D. Rajan had believed that Sajan and he would be working together.
sourceAnswer: D. Rajan had believed that Sajan and he would be working together.

parsed.stem:
Rajan was not happy that Sajan decided to do the project on his own. On observing his unhappiness, Sajan explained to Rajan that he preferred to work independently.

Which one of the statements below is logically valid and can be inferred from the above sentences?

options.text: Rajan has decided to work only in a group.
Rajan and Sajan were formed into a group against their wishes.
Sajan had decided to give a part of the project to Rajan.
Rajan had believed that Sajan and he would be working together.

sourceQuestion:
Moving into a world of big data will require us to change our thinking about the merits of exactitude. To apply the conventional mindset of measurement to the digital, connected world of the twenty-first century is to miss a crucial point. As mentioned earlier, the obsession with exactness is an artefact of the information-deprived analog era. When data was sparse, every data point was critical, and thus care was taken to avoid letting any point bias the analysis. From 'BIG DATA' by Viktor Mayer-Schonberger and Kenneth Cukier.

Which of the following can be logically inferred from the above passage?

A. Emperors who do not leave significant sculpted evidence are completely forgotten.
B. Adora produced stone carved edicts to ensure that later historians will respect him.
C. Exactitude is not critical in dealing with big data.
D. Diverse data leads to a bias in the analysis.
sourceAnswer: D. Diverse data leads to a bias in the analysis.

parsed.stem:
Moving into a world of big data will require us to change our thinking about the merits of exactitude. To apply the conventional mindset of measurement to the digital, connected world of the twenty-first century is to miss a crucial point. As mentioned earlier, the obsession with exactness is an artefact of the information-deprived analog era. When data was sparse, every data point was critical, and thus care was taken to avoid letting any point bias the analysis. From 'BIG DATA' by Viktor Mayer-Schonberger and Kenneth Cukier.

Which of the following can be logically inferred from the above passage?

options.text: Emperors who do not leave significant sculpted evidence are completely forgotten.
Adora produced stone carved edicts to ensure that later historians will respect him.
Exactitude is not critical in dealing with big data.
Diverse data leads to a bias in the analysis.

sourceQuestion:
For submitting tax returns, all resident males with annual income below Rs 10 lakh should fill up Form P and all resident females with income below Rs 8 lakh should fill up Form Q. All people with incomes above Rs 10 lakh should fill up Form R, except non-residents with income above Rs 15 lakh, who should fill up Form S. All others should fill up Form T.

Which of the following should fill up Form T?

A. A resident with annual income Rs 9 lakh.
B. A resident female with annual income Rs 9 lakh.
C. A non-resident with annual income Rs 16 lakh.
D. A non-resident with annual income Rs 8 lakh.
sourceAnswer: B. A resident female with annual income Rs 9 lakh.

parsed.stem:
For submitting tax returns, all resident males with annual income below Rs 10 lakh should fill up Form P and all resident females with income below Rs 8 lakh should fill up Form Q. All people with incomes above Rs 10 lakh should fill up Form R, except non-residents with income above Rs 15 lakh, who should fill up Form S. All others should fill up Form T.

Which of the following should fill up Form T?

options.text: A resident with annual income Rs 9 lakh.
A resident female with annual income Rs 9 lakh.
A non-resident with annual income Rs 16 lakh.
A non-resident with annual income Rs 8 lakh.

sourceQuestion:
Read the following paragraph and choose the correct statement.

Climate change has reduced human security and threatened human well being. An ignored reality of human progress is that human security largely depends upon environmental security. But on the contrary, human progress seems contradictory to environmental security. To keep up with the required level is a challenge to be addressed by one and all. One of the ways to curb the climate change may be suitable scientific innovations, while the other may be the implementation of strict laws.

A. Human progress and security are positively associated with environmental security.
B. Human progress is contradictory to environmental security.
C. Human security is contradictory to environmental security.
D. Human progress depends upon environmental security.
sourceAnswer: B. Human progress is contradictory to environmental security.

parsed.stem:
Read the following paragraph and choose the correct statement.

Climate change has reduced human security and threatened human well being. An ignored reality of human progress is that human security largely depends upon environmental security. But on the contrary, human progress seems contradictory to environmental security. To keep up with the required level is a challenge to be addressed by one and all. One of the ways to curb the climate change may be suitable scientific innovations, while the other may be the implementation of strict laws.

options.text: Human progress and security are positively associated with environmental security.
Human progress is contradictory to environmental security.
Human security is contradictory to environmental security.
Human progress depends upon environmental security.

sourceQuestion:
Ms. X will be in Bagdogra from 01/05/2014 to 21/05/2014 and from 22/05/2014 to 31/05/2014. On the morning of 22/05/2014, she will reach Kochi via Mumbai.

Which one of the statements below is logically valid and can be inferred from the above sentences?

A. Ms. X will be in Kochi for one day, only in May.
B. Ms. X will be in Kochi for only one day in May.
C. Ms. X will be in Kochi for only one day.
D. Only Ms. X will be in Kochi for one day in May.
sourceAnswer: D. Only Ms. X will be in Kochi for one day in May.

Which one of the statements below is logically valid and can be inferred from the above sentences?

options.text: Ms. X will be in Kochi for one day, only in May.
Ms. X will be in Kochi for only one day in May.
Ms. X will be in Kochi for only one day.
Only Ms. X will be in Kochi for one day in May.

sourceQuestion:
Today, we consider Ashoka as a great ruler because of the copious evidence he left behind in the form of stone carved edicts. Historians tend to correlate greatness of a king at his time with the availability of evidence today. Which of the following can be logically inferred from the above sentences?

A. Emperors who do not leave significant sculpted evidence are completely forgotten.
B. Ashoka produced stone carved edicts to ensure that later historians will respect him.
C. Statues of kings are good for their memory and the preservation of their history.
D. A king's greatness, as we know him today, is interpreted by historians.
sourceAnswer: D. A king's greatness, as we know him today, is interpreted by historians.

parsed.stem: Today, we consider Ashoka as a great ruler because of the copious evidence he left behind in the form of stone carved edicts. Historians tend to correlate greatness of a king at his time with the availability of evidence today. Which of the following can be logically inferred from the above sentences?

options.text: Emperors who do not leave significant sculpted evidence are completely forgotten.
Ashoka produced stone carved edicts to ensure that later historians will respect him.
Statues of kings are good for their memory and the preservation of their history.
A king's greatness, as we know him today, is interpreted by historians.

sourceQuestion:
Social science disciplines were in existence in an amorphous form until the colonial period when they were institutionalized. In varying degrees, they were intended to further the colonial interest. In the time of globalization and the economic rise of postcolonial countries like India, conventional ways of knowledge production have become obsolete.

Which of the following can be logically inferred from the above passage?

(i) Social science disciplines have a colonial origin.
(ii) Social science disciplines are still being pursued in the postcolonial countries.

A. (i) only
B. (ii) only
C. (i) and (ii)
D. Neither (i) nor (ii)
sourceAnswer: A. (i) only

parsed.stem:

In the time of globalization and the economic rise of postcolonial countries like India, conventional ways of knowledge production have become obsolete.

Which of the following can be logically inferred from the above passage?

(i) Social science disciplines have a colonial origin.
(ii) Social science disciplines are still being pursued in the postcolonial countries.

options.text: (i) only
(ii) only
(i) and (ii)
Neither (i) nor (ii)

sourceQuestion:
The overwhelming number of people infected with rabies in India has been flagged by the World Health Organization as a source of concern. It is estimated that inoculating 70% of pets and stray dogs against rabies can lead to a significant reduction in the number of people infected with rabies.

Which of the following can be most logically inferred from the above sentences?

A. The number of people in India infected with rabies is high.
B. The number of people in other parts of the world infected with rabies is low.
C. (i) and (ii) only.
D. (ii) and (iv) only.
sourceAnswer: A. The number of people in India infected with rabies is high.

parsed.stem:
The overwhelming number of people infected with rabies in India has been flagged by the World Health Organization as a source of concern. It is estimated that inoculating 70% of pets and stray dogs against rabies can lead to a significant reduction in the number of people infected with rabies.

Which of the following can be most logically inferred from the above sentences?

options.text: The number of people in India infected with rabies is high.
The number of people in other parts of the world infected with rabies is low.
(i) and (ii) only.
(ii) and (iv) only.

sourceQuestion:
The old concert hall was demolished because of fears that the foundation would be affected by the construction of the new metro line in the area. Modern technology for underground metro construction tried to mitigate the impact of pressurized air pockets created by the excavation of large amounts of soil. But even with these safeguards, it was feared that the concert hall would not be stable.

From this, one can infer that:

A. the foundations of old buildings create pressurized air pockets underground, which are difficult to handle during metro construction.
B. metro construction has to be done carefully considering its impact on the foundations of existing buildings.
C. construction in an area from an impossible hurdle to metro construction in that area.
D. pressurized air can be used to excavate large amount of soil from underground areas.
sourceAnswer: B. metro construction has to be done carefully considering its impact on the foundations of existing buildings.

parsed.stem:
The old concert hall was demolished because of fears that the foundation would be affected by the construction of the new metro line in the area. Modern technology for underground metro construction tried to mitigate the impact of pressurized air pockets created by the excavation of large amounts of soil. But even with these safeguards, it was feared that the concert hall would not be stable.

From this, one can infer that:

options.text: the foundations of old buildings create pressurized air pockets underground, which are difficult to handle during metro construction.
metro construction has to be done carefully considering its impact on the foundations of existing buildings.
construction in an area from an impossible hurdle to metro construction in that area.
pressurized air can be used to excavate large amount of soil from underground areas.

sourceQuestion:
Bhaichung was observing the pattern of people entering and leaving a car service centre. There was a single window where customers were being served. He saw that people inevitably came out of the centre in the order that they went in. However, the time they spent inside seemed to vary a lot: some people came out in a matter of minutes while others took a lot longer.

From this, what can one conclude?

A. The centre operates on a first-come-first-served basis, but with variable service times, depending on specific customers.
B. Customers were served in an arbitrary order, since they took varying amounts of time for service completion in the centre.
C. The time spent by a person inside the service centre was not ultimately associated with the actors, to get the perspective needed for imperial recording of matters.
D. Actors are typically associated with the imperial recording of matters.
sourceAnswer: A. The centre operates on a first-come-first-served basis, but with variable service times, depending on specific customers.

parsed.stem:

From this, what can one conclude?

options.text: The centre operates on a first-come-first-served basis, but with variable service times, depending on specific customers.
Customers were served in an arbitrary order, since they took varying amounts of time for service completion in the centre.
The time spent by a person inside the service centre was not ultimately associated with the actors, to get the perspective needed for imperial recording of matters.
Actors are typically associated with the imperial recording of matters.

sourceQuestion:
A map shows the elevations of Darjeeling, Gangtok, Kalimpong, Pelling, and Siliguri. Kalimpong is at a lower elevation than Gangtok. Pelling is at a higher elevation than Gangtok. Pelling is at a higher elevation than Kalimpong. Siliguri is at a lower elevation than Darjeeling. Darjeeling is at a lower elevation than Gangtok. Siliguri is at a lower elevation than Kalimpong.

Which of the following statements can be inferred from the paragraph above?

i. Pelling is at a higher elevation than Siliguri.
ii. Kalimpong is at a lower elevation than Darjeeling.
iii. Darjeeling is at a lower elevation than Kalimpong.
iv. Siliguri is at a lower elevation than Gangtok.

A. Only i.
B. Only ii and iii.
C. Only i and iv.
D. Only ii and iv.
sourceAnswer: C. Only i and iv.

parsed.stem:
A map shows the elevations of Darjeeling, Gangtok, Kalimpong, Pelling, and Siliguri. Kalimpong is at a lower elevation than Gangtok. Pelling is at a higher elevation than Gangtok. Pelling is at a higher elevation than Kalimpong. Siliguri is at a lower elevation than Darjeeling. Darjeeling is at a lower elevation than Gangtok. Siliguri is at a lower elevation than Kalimpong.

Which of the following statements can be inferred from the paragraph above?

i. Pelling is at a higher elevation than Siliguri.
ii. Kalimpong is at a lower elevation than Darjeeling.
iii. Darjeeling is at a lower elevation than Kalimpong.
iv. Siliguri is at a lower elevation than Gangtok.

options.text: Only i.
Only ii and iii.
Only i and iv.
Only ii and iv.

sourceQuestion:
Read the following passage and answer the question.

"If you are looking for a history of India, or for an account of the rise and fall of the British Raj, or for all reason of the cleaving of the subcontinent into two mutually antagonistic parts and the effects this mutilation will have in the respective sections, and ultimately on Asia, you will not find it in these pages; for though I have spent a lifetime in the country, I lived too near the seat of events, and was too intimately associated with the actors, to get the perspective needed for impartial recording of these matters."

Which of the following statements best reflects the author's opinion?

A. An intimate association does not allow for the necessary perspective.
B. Matters are recorded with an impartial perspective.
C. An intimate association offers an impartial perspective.
D. Actors are typically associated with the impartial recording of matters.
sourceAnswer: A. An intimate association does not allow for the necessary perspective.

parsed.stem:
Read the following passage and answer the question.

"If you are looking for a history of India, or for an account of the rise and fall of the British Raj, or for all reason of the cleaving of the subcontinent into two mutually antagonistic parts and the effects this mutilation will have in the respective sections, and ultimately on Asia, you will not find it in these pages; for though I have spent a lifetime in the country, I lived too near the seat of events, and was too intimately associated with the actors, to get the perspective needed for impartial recording of these matters."

Which of the following statements best reflects the author's opinion?

options.text: An intimate association does not allow for the necessary perspective.
Matters are recorded with an impartial perspective.
An intimate association offers an impartial perspective.
Actors are typically associated with the impartial recording of matters.

sourceQuestion:
Read the following passage and answer the question.

"Here, throughout the early 1920s, Stuart continued to fight his losing battle to allow his sepoys to wear their caste-marks and their own choice of facial hair on parade, being again reprimanded by the commander-in-chief. His retort that 'A stronger instance than this of European prejudice with relation to this country has never come under my observations' had no effect on his superiors."

Which of the following statements is most accurate based on the above paragraph?

A. Stuart's commander-in-chief was moved by this demonstration of his prejudice.
B. The Europeans were accommodating of the sepoy's desire to wear their caste-marks.
C. Stuart's losing battle refers to his inability to succeed in enabling sepoys to wear caste-marks.
D. The commander-in-chief was exempt from the European prejudice that dictated how the sepoys were to dress.
sourceAnswer: C. Stuart's losing battle refers to his inability to succeed in enabling sepoys to wear caste-marks.

parsed.stem:
Read the following passage and answer the question.

"Here, throughout the early 1920s, Stuart continued to fight his losing battle to allow his sepoys to wear their caste-marks and their own choice of facial hair on parade, being again reprimanded by the commander-in-chief. His retort that 'A stronger instance than this of European prejudice with relation to this country has never come under my observations' had no effect on his superiors."

Which of the following statements is most accurate based on the above paragraph?

options.text: Stuart's commander-in-chief was moved by this demonstration of his prejudice.
The Europeans were accommodating of the sepoy's desire to wear their caste-marks.
Stuart's losing battle refers to his inability to succeed in enabling sepoys to wear caste-marks.
The commander-in-chief was exempt from the European prejudice that dictated how the sepoys were to dress.

sourceQuestion:
A coastal region with unparalleled beauty is home to many species of animals. It is dotted with coral reefs and unspoilt white sandy beaches. It has remained inaccessible to tourists due to poor connectivity and lack of accommodation. A company has spotted the opportunity and is planning to develop a luxury resort with helicopter service to the nearest major city airport. Environmentalists are upset that this would lead to the region becoming crowded and polluted like any other major beach resorts.

Which one of the following statements can be logically inferred from the information given in the above paragraph?

A. The culture and tradition of the local people will be influenced by the tourists.
B. The region will become crowded and polluted due to tourism.
C. The coral reefs are on the decline and could soon vanish.
D. Helicopter connectivity would lead to an increase in tourists coming to the region.
sourceAnswer: D. Helicopter connectivity would lead to an increase in tourists coming to the region.

parsed.stem:
A coastal region with unparalleled beauty is home to many species of animals. It is dotted with coral reefs and unspoilt white sandy beaches. It has remained inaccessible to tourists due to poor connectivity and lack of accommodation. A company has spotted the opportunity and is planning to develop a luxury resort with helicopter service to the nearest major city airport. Environmentalists are upset that this would lead to the region becoming crowded and polluted like any other major beach resorts.

Which one of the following statements can be logically inferred from the information given in the above paragraph?

options.text: The culture and tradition of the local people will be influenced by the tourists.
The region will become crowded and polluted due to tourism.
The coral reefs are on the decline and could soon vanish.
Helicopter connectivity would lead to an increase in tourists coming to the region.

sourceQuestion:
The Cricket Board has long recognized John's potential as a leader of the team. However, his on-field temper has always been a matter of concern for them since his junior days. While this aggression has filled stadia with die-hard fans, it has always stopped John from converting his raw aggression into big scores. Over the past three seasons though, that picture of John has been replaced by a cerebral, calculative and successful batsman-captain. After many years, it appears that the team has finally found a complete captain.

Which of the following statements can be logically inferred from the above paragraph?

i. Even as a junior cricketer, John was considered a good captain.
ii. Finding a complete captain is a challenge.
iii. Fans and the Cricket Board have differing views on what they want in a captain.
iv. Over the past three seasons, John has rectified his bowling.

A. (i), (ii) and (iii) only
B. (i) and (iii) only
C. (ii) and (iii) only
D. (i), (ii), (iii) and (iv)
sourceAnswer: C. (ii) and (iii) only

parsed.stem:
The Cricket Board has long recognized John's potential as a leader of the team. However, his on-field temper has always been a matter of concern for them since his junior days. While this aggression has filled stadia with die-hard fans, it has always stopped John from converting his raw aggression into big scores. Over the past three seasons though, that picture of John has been replaced by a cerebral, calculative and successful batsman-captain. After many years, it appears that the team has finally found a complete captain.

Which of the following statements can be logically inferred from the above paragraph?

i. Even as a junior cricketer, John was considered a good captain.
ii. Finding a complete captain is a challenge.
iii. Fans and the Cricket Board have differing views on what they want in a captain.
iv. Over the past three seasons, John has rectified his bowling.

options.text: (i), (ii) and (iii) only
(i) and (iii) only
(ii) and (iii) only
(i), (ii), (iii) and (iv)

sourceQuestion:
The new cotton technology, Bollgard II, with herbicide-tolerant traits has developed into a thriving business in India. However, the commercial use of this technology is not legal in India. Notwithstanding that, reports indicate that the herbicide tolerant Bt cotton had been purchased by farmers at an average price of Rs 200 more than the control price of ordinary cotton, and had been planted in 15% of the cotton growing area in the 2017 Kharif season.

Which one of the following statements can be inferred from the given passage?

A. Farmers want to access the new technology if India benefits from it.
B. Farmers want to access the new technology even if it is not legal.
C. Farmers want to access the new technology for experimental purposes.
D. Farmers want to access the new technology by paying high price.
sourceAnswer: B. Farmers want to access the new technology even if it is not legal.

parsed.stem:
The new cotton technology, Bollgard II, with herbicide-tolerant traits has developed into a thriving business in India. However, the commercial use of this technology is not legal in India. Notwithstanding that, reports indicate that the herbicide tolerant Bt cotton had been purchased by farmers at an average price of Rs 200 more than the control price of ordinary cotton, and had been planted in 15% of the cotton growing area in the 2017 Kharif season.

Which one of the following statements can be inferred from the given passage?

options.text: Farmers want to access the new technology if India benefits from it.
Farmers want to access the new technology even if it is not legal.
Farmers want to access the new technology for experimental purposes.
Farmers want to access the new technology by paying high price.

sourceQuestion:
"Popular Hindi fiction, despite - or perhaps because of - its wide reach, often does not appear in our cinema. As ideals that viewers are meant to look up to rather identify with, Hindi film protagonists usually read books of aspirational value: textbook, English books, or high-value literature."

Which one of the following CANNOT be inferred from the above passage?

A. Though popular Hindi fiction has wide reach, it often does not appear in the movies.
B. Protagonists in Hindi movies, being ideals for viewers, read only books of aspirational value.
C. Textbooks, English books, or high-value literature have aspirational value.
D. People do not look up to writers of books or high-value literature.
sourceAnswer: B, D

parsed.stem:
"Popular Hindi fiction, despite - or perhaps because of - its wide reach, often does not appear in our cinema. As ideals that viewers are meant to look up to rather identify with, Hindi film protagonists usually read books of aspirational value: textbook, English books, or high-value literature."

Which one of the following CANNOT be inferred from the above passage?

options.text: Though popular Hindi fiction has wide reach, it often does not appear in the movies.
Protagonists in Hindi movies, being ideals for viewers, read only books of aspirational value.
Textbooks, English books, or high-value literature have aspirational value.
People do not look up to writers of books or high-value literature.

sourceQuestion:
What is the logical translation of the following statement? "None of my friends are perfect."
Let F(x) denote 'x is my friend' and P(x) denote 'x is perfect'.

A. forall x (F(x) -> ~P(x))
B. ~exists x (F(x) ^ P(x))
C. exists x (F(x) ^ ~P(x))
D. ~exists x (F(x) ^ P(x))
sourceAnswer: D. ~exists x (F(x) ^ P(x))

parsed.stem:
What is the logical translation of the following statement? "None of my friends are perfect."
Let F(x) denote 'x is my friend' and P(x) denote 'x is perfect'.

options.text: forall x (F(x) -> ~P(x))
~exists x (F(x) ^ P(x))
exists x (F(x) ^ ~P(x))
~exists x (F(x) ^ P(x))

sourceQuestion:
The Newspaper reports that over 500 hectares of tribal land spread across 28 tribal settlements in Mohinitampuram forest division have already been 'alienated'. A top forest official said, 'First the tribals are duped out of their land holdings. Second, the families thus rendered landless are often forced to encroach further into the forests.'

On the basis of the information in the paragraph, _______ is/are responsible for duping the tribals.

A. forest officials
B. Newspaper
C. The Newspaper
D. landless families
sourceAnswer: D. landless families

On the basis of the information in the paragraph, _______ is/are responsible for duping the tribals.

options.text: forest officials
Newspaper
The Newspaper
landless families

sourceQuestion:
'Indian history was written by British historians — extremely well documented and researched, but not always impartial. History had to serve its purpose: Everything was made subservient to the glory of the Union Jack. Latter-day Indian scholars presented a contrary picture.'

From the text above, we can infer that

A. British history was well documented and researched but was sometimes biased
B. British history was not well documented and researched but was always biased
C. Indian history was well documented and researched and was sometimes biased
D. Indian history was well documented and researched and was always biased
sourceAnswer: C. Indian history was well documented and researched and was sometimes biased

parsed.stem:
'Indian history was written by British historians — extremely well documented and researched, but not always impartial. History had to serve its purpose: Everything was made subservient to the glory of the Union Jack. Latter-day Indian scholars presented a contrary picture.'

From the text above, we can infer that

options.text: British history was well documented and researched but was sometimes biased
British history was not well documented and researched but was always biased
Indian history was well documented and researched and was sometimes biased
Indian history was well documented and researched and was always biased

sourceQuestion:
An award-winning study by a group of researchers suggests that men are as prone to buying on impulse as women but women feel more guilty about shopping.

Which one of the following statements can be inferred from the given text?

A. Some men and women indulge in buying on impulse
B. All men and women indulge in buying on impulse
C. Few men and women indulge in buying on impulse
D. Many men and women indulge in buying on impulse
sourceAnswer: A. Some men and women indulge in buying on impulse

options.text: Some men and women indulge in buying on impulse
All men and women indulge in buying on impulse
Few men and women indulge in buying on impulse
Many men and women indulge in buying on impulse

sourceQuestion:
'I read somewhere that in ancient times the prestige of a kingdom depended upon the number of taxes that it was able to levy on its people. It was very much like the prestige of a head-hunter in his own community.'

Based on the paragraph above, the prestige of a head-hunter depended upon

A. the prestige of the kingdom
B. the prestige of the heads
C. the number of heads he could collect
D. the number of heads he could gather
sourceAnswer: D. the number of heads he could gather

options.text: the prestige of the kingdom
the prestige of the heads
the number of heads he could collect

sourceQuestion:
The nomenclature of Hindustani music has changed over the centuries. Since the medieval period, dhrupad styles were identified as baanis. Terms like gayaki and baaj were used to refer to vocal and instrumental styles, respectively. With the institutionalization of music education, the term gharana became acceptable. Gharana originally referred to hereditary musicians from a particular family, including disciples and grand disciples.

Which one of the following pairings is NOT correct?

A. dhrupad, baani
B. gayaki, vocal
C. baaj, institution
D. gharana, lineage
sourceAnswer: C. baaj, institution

parsed.stem:

Terms like gayaki and baaj were used to refer to vocal and instrumental styles, respectively. With the institutionalization of music education, the term gharana became acceptable. Gharana originally referred to hereditary musicians from a particular family, including disciples and grand disciples.

Which one of the following pairings is NOT correct?

options.text: dhrupad, baani
gayaki, vocal
baaj, institution
gharana, lineage

sourceQuestion:
Congo was named by Europeans. Congo's dictator Mobutu later changed the name of the country and the river to Zaire with the objective of Africanising names of persons and spaces. However, the name Zaire was a Portuguese alteration of Nzadi o Nzere, a local African term meaning 'River that swallows Rivers'. Zaire was the Portuguese name for the Congo river in the 16th and 17th centuries.

Which one of the following statements can be inferred from the paragraph above?

A. Mobutu was not aware of the Portuguese alteration of the local African term
B. The term Nzadi o Nzere was of Portuguese origin
C. Mobutu's desire to Africanise names was undermined by the Portuguese
D. As a dictator Mobutu ordered the Portuguese to alter the name of the river to Zaire
sourceAnswer: A. Mobutu was not aware of the Portuguese alteration of the local African term

Zaire was the Portuguese name for the Congo river in the 16th and 17th centuries.

The term Nzadi o Nzere was of Portuguese origin
